ety: Zend dusmainyu, i.e. dush+m˚; S. dush+manyu
def: s.m. Enemy, foe, adversary (in polite speech the word is frequently used euphemistically in a peculiar manner, e.g. āp-ke dushmanoṅ-kī tabʻīyat kab-se ʻalīl hai, 'How long have your foes been indisposed?' but implying, 'Since when have you (or has your honour) been indisposed?')
more: n. m. P. dusht bad, man mind, S. दुर्मनाः from दुः wicked, and मनः mind. 1. An enemy ( bairī , 1). 1. Āo, jāo, ghar tumhārā! khānā māṅge dushman hamārā . Prov. My house is yours, come when you please or go, but whoso wants to eat becomes my foe. 2. Udhār dīje, dushman kīje . Prov. Give a loan, an enemy own. 3. Dushmanoṅ ke muṅh meṅ khāk! May my enemies be choked! (May they be powerless!). 4. Dost kā dushman, dushman; dushman kā dushman, dost . Prov. Your friends' foes are your foes, your foe's foes are your friends. 2. Pl. ( In polite language ) Yourself; your honored self; your worship. Āpke dushmanoṅ kī tabiyat kab se alīl hai? jānī dushman , Mortal enemy. ٴaql kā dushman , A stupid man; a fool (احمق).

s. m. f. An enemy, adversary, foe. دشمن جاني dushman-i-jānī, A deadly foe. دشمن کام dushman-kām, A calamity (q. d. the wish of an enemy). دشمن کشي dushman-kush-ī, Killing of an enemy. دشمن گداز dushman-gudāz, Dissolver or destroyer of enemies.
subs: s.m. Dissolver or destroyer of enemies
s.m. 'Foe of knowledge,' a stupid person, a fool.

def: s.f. = دتي duti, q.v. s.f. Light; brilliance, splendour, beauty; — spite, malice.
more: intj. Tut! away! begone! Dut! tere kī. dūt dabak , n. f. Rebuke; reproof; reprimand. dūt dabak k., dutkārnā , v. a. To chide; reprove.
1. ( दूर ) interj. Away! begone! avaunt! 2. or duti ( द्युति ) s. f. Splendour, light, beauty. 3. Spite, malice. دت دبک dut dabak, Reproof, reprimand.

def: s.m. Messenger, carrier of intelligence, envoy, emissary, ambassador; a go-between, negotiator; an angel who passes between God and man; a secret messenger, a spy.
more: n. m. S. दूत from दु to go, Pālī. dūtako, duto . 1. An ambassador; envoy; a delegate. 2. A secret messenger; an emissary. Khet bigāṛe kharatuā, aur sabhā bigāṛe dūt . Prov. As weeds the field destroy, so spies good company. dūt-pan , n. m. 1. See jāsūsī . 2. Mischief-making. dūtā'ī , n. f. Embassy.
s. m. An ambassadour, messenger, a go-between, an angel who passes between God and man.

def: intj. Away! begone! avaunt!
subs: s.f. = dut-kār, s.m. = dut-kārī, s.f. Driving away; rebuke, reproof, reprimand
or dat-kārnā, v.t. To drive away; to reprove, rebuke, reprimand.

def: adj. Double, twice, bent.
s.f. Embassy, message; the status, employment, or office of an envoy, &c.
s.m. (-ikā), A messenger, envoy, &c. ( = dūt).
v.t. To repress, keep down or under, subdue, to snub, chide; to threaten (cf. ḍāṅṭnā).
more: v. a. 1. To press down. 2. To snub, to chide, to keep under.

def: s.f. = S دوتتو दूतत्व dūtatva, s.m. The business or office of an envoy, or ambassador, &c., the condition of a messenger or emissary.
s.m. = S دوتتا दूतता dūtatā, s.f. The business or office of an envoy, or ambassador, &c., the condition of a messenger or emissary.
s.f. Light; brilliance, splendour, beauty; — spite, malice.
s.f. = dūti or dūtī, q.v. s.f. Light; brilliance, splendour, beauty; — spite, malice.
s.f. A female messenger, a confidante, a go-between, a procuress, a bawd; a spy; a gossiping, mischief-making woman, a tale-bearer, mischief-maker.
more: n. f. 1. A female messenger, or spy. 2. A go-between; a procuress ( kuṭnī ). 3. A backbiter. 4. An incendiary; a firebrand. 1. Sās morī dūtī, nanad morī bairan . My mother-in-law's a spy, my sister-in-law my enemy. 2. Dūlī kahe, 'sun re, man-mohan, paṅkh binā maiṅ pakherū uṛāūṅ, Kāgaj kī dūṅ nāo ko sāgar, dārū ke ganj meṅ āg chhipāūṅ, Jal meṅ thal karūṅ, aur pahāṛ meṅ nāo chalāë dikhāūṅ, Jo tiryā paulī nahīṅ jhāṅkat vā ko thelī bajāvāt lāūṅ .' Kabit. The bawd says, 'witching Mohan hear! a wingless bird I fly, Paper I float as an ocean boat, fires hid in powder lie, In water I away can make, on hills a sail you 'll spy, A woman who ne'er looked outside a strumpet's trade shall ply.'
s. f. 1. ( दूती ) A bawd. 2. ( द्युति ) Refulgence.

def: s.f. See دتي duti, and dyuti.
s.f. = dūtī, q.v. s.f. Light; brilliance, splendour, beauty; — spite, malice.
adj. Second, the second; — the second, or accusative case.
more: adj. S. द्वितीया, Pālī, dutiyā . 1. Second ( dūsrā ). Eko Brahm, dutiyo nāstī . One God, no other. 2. ( Gram .) The objective case ( karm ). duit-bhāo , n. m. Inequality; unfairness.

def: s. m. Reproof.
s. f. A reproof.
v. a. To reprove.

ety: fem. of dūt; — nī = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. = dūtī, q.v. s.f. Light; brilliance, splendour, beauty; — spite, malice.

ety: dūt+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. The condition of a messenger or envoy, &c. ( = dūtatā); ambassadorship; — spying, espionage (syn. jāsūsī); — mischief-making.
v. a. 1. To drive away scornfully. 2. (see دهتا dhuttā ) To deceive, to balk, to cheat.
s.m. Trick, deception, fraud
s.m. Driving away (by saying dat, or dut-dut)
more: n. m. Cheat; fraud ( buttā ). duttā denā , v. a. To cheat ( buttā denā ).

ety: fr. دور 'to move or turn round'
def: s.m. Whirl, giddiness in the head, vertigo.
wety: From Sanskrit द्वार (dvāra), from Proto-Indo-Iranian, from Proto-Indo-European *dʰworom (“enclosure, courtyard, i.e. something enclosed by the door”), from *dʰwor-, compare *dʰwer-.

ety: Pehl. dvāz-dah; Zend dva-daśan; S. dvā-daśan
def: adj. Twelve
subs: The twelve signs of the zodiac.

def: s.f. An aggregate of twelve, a a dozen.

ety: Pehl. dvāz-dahum; Zend dva-daśa; S. dvā-daśa
def: adj. The twelfth.
adj. & adv. Two and two, by twos; being two-fold or double, binary.
more: n. f. 1. A second; another. Jāṛe meṅ rūī yā dūī . Prov. For winter a blanket or a bedfellow. 2. Matching; pairing. 3. Duality. 3. Disunion; enmity.

Mah. n. f. S. दर्वी A large wooden spoon or ladle. 1. Haṅḍyā na ḍoī, muā sārī pat khoī! Prov. Nor pot nor spoon, shamed by this loon! (She is despised for her poverty). 2. Jiske hāth ḍoī uskā sab koī . Prov. Who hath the spoon that serves the food will be by every body wooed. 3. Ḍoīna māro, bachche ko haukā ho jāëgā . Mah. Wom. Dont strike the child with the spoon, he will be hunger-sick. (Contact with the spoon, which is in constant request for stirring the pot, will make the child continually hungry).
1. adj. By twos, being twofold or double, binary. 2. s. f. The being two or twofold; disunion: see بيگانگي begāna-gī.

def: adj. Second; inferior, second sort.
more: n. m. ḍomnī , n. f. In the East, a low caste who make ropes, mats, chicks, baskets, fans, etc., and are also employed in removing dead bodies and carcases. They are generally thieves. In the West the ḍom is a musician, and the female ḍomnī , otherwise called Mīrāsin , is an actress and singer, who performs in private before the women of the family. 1. Sarāhal bahuṛiyā ḍom ghar jāë . Prov. The daughter-in-law so famed for chastity goes to the ḍom's house secretly. 2. Bin bulāī ḍomnī, laṛke bāle sāth āë . Prov. The uninvited songstress see with all her children squatted free. ḍom-panā , n. 1. The arts practised by ḍoms . 2. Adulation ( bhaṭaī , 2, 3).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُوُم (duwum).

def: adj. = duvum, q.v. adj. Second; inferior, second sort.
adj. Second; next, &c. (see dūsrā); inferior, secondary; — adv. In the second place or instance, secondly.
more: Opp. of اول 1. Second; next; secondly. 2. Inferior; secondary; second sort or quality.

def: adj. = duyum, duvumīn.

ety: old P. dusdahā; Zend duzhdāo, dush ( = S. dush)+dāo, 'knowledge,' &c., rt. dā
def: s.m. A thief, robber.
more: s. m. A thief, a robber.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُزْد (duzd), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *duš- (“bad, wrong”).

def: = H دوادسي द्वादसी dvā-dasī, vulg. duʼādsī, See dvā-daś; and dvā-daśī.
s.f. The twelfth day of the half month (reckoned from new or full moon).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Double, two-fold; of two sorts; — double existence or nature, two-fold state or form, duality; division or separation into two parts; disunion, diversity, difference, variance, contest, conflict; duplicity; doubt, uncertainty; contradiction, dispute
subs: s.m. Two-fold form or state, double nature, duality, duplicity; doubt, uncertainty; sowing dissension between, or causing the separation of, allies; division of an army
s.m. A making into two, separating, disuniting.

def: adj. Of or relating to an island; inhabiting an island; — s.m. An islander.
s.m. Duality, doubling, the being doubled, duplication; dualism (in Philosophy), the assertion of two principles (as of life and soul, spirit and matter, God and the universe, &c.); doubt; separation
subs: s.m. 'An asserter of dualism,' a philosopher who asserts the two principles or the existence of the soul as separate from the Supreme Being.

def: adj. Of or belonging to heaven or to divine things, heavenly, divine, supernatural.
s.m. The state of being two-fold, duality, two-fold manner or character; duplicity; diversity; variance.

s.m. A pair, brace, couple; a couple of animals of different sexes, male and female; union of the sexes, coupline; any two things taken together, any pair of qualities and conditions which are the opposite of one another (as heat and cold; happiness and misery, &c.)

s.m. A pair, brace, couple; a couple of animals of different sexes, male and female; union of the sexes, coupline; any two things taken together, any pair of qualities and conditions which are the opposite of one another (as heat and cold; happiness and misery, &c.)

s.m. That die, or side of a die, which is marked with two spots or points; 'the age with the number two' (dwā), the third of the four yugas or ages of the world (comprising 2400 years); — doubt, uncertainty.
more: (Doubt) s. m. The third or brazen age, comprising 864,000 years.

def: s.m.f. Door, gate; doorway, gateway, passage, entrance, opening; access, way; vehicle, means, expedient, opportunity, medium (by which anything is effected)

s.f. 'The city with many gates'; name of a sacred place of the Hindūs situated near the mouth of the gulf of Kach, on the western coast of Hindūstān (it was the capital of Krishṇa, and is supposed to have been submerged by the sea)
more: s. f. Name of a Hindū holy place on the shore of the western sea, near the mouth of the gulf of Cutch. دوارکا ناتهـ द्वारिकानाथ dwārikā-nāth, Master of dwārikā, i. e. Kr̤ish̤ṇa.

more: adj. S. एकः Pr. ekham, eam ; P. yak . 1. One; a; an; only; only one; a certain one; each; each one; another. 1. Ek anḍā woh bhī gandā . Prov. Only one egg, and that an addled egg. (Said of a bad and only child). 2. Chalo, sakhī, tahāṅ chaleṅ, tahāṅ mileṅ Brij-Rāj, Go-ras bechat Hari mileṅ, ek panth do kāj . Rās Līlā. Come, sister, let us go where we the Lord of Birj shall meet, We'll sell our milk and Harī meet, one stroke. two objects hit. 3. Ek se ek, do se gyārah . Prov. One is but one, from two eleven count on. (One is one, but one next to one is eleven, and it requires two factors to make a product.) 4. Kamalan ko rab ek hai, rab ko kamal anek, Tum ko ham se bahut haiṅ, ham ko tum sā ek. Dohā . The many lotus flowers one sun alone have found, One sun doth many lotus flowers own, For you how many a one like me abound, For me none such as thee, the only One. 5. Ek na shud, do shud . P…

adj. One; only. ايگ آد ek ād, A half, a part, some, some few. ايک بار ek bār or ايک باري ek bār-ī or ايک بارگي ek bārgī, Once, all at once. ايک دست ek dast, Altogether, entire. ايک دل ek dil, Unanimous, of one mind. ايک سان ek sāṅ, adj. Alike, similar, identical. ايک سر ek sar, All at once, all together. ايک سي دن نرهني ek se din na rahne, Is used to denote a change of condition, most commonly from good to evil, but sometimes the reverse. ايک کي دس سناني ek kī das sunānī, To reply ten (words) for one (spoken of a person who replies with many words, especially in giving abuse). ايک قلم ek ḳalam, or ايک لخت ek laḵẖt, All together, entirely, at once. ايک لوتا ek-lautā, adj. Only, single (child without brother or sister). ايک نه ايک ek na ek, One or other.

more: n. m. 1. Unanimity; union; concurrence; accord; concord. 2. Alliance; league; compact; combination; conspiracy. Saboṅ ne ekā kar līyā . 3. Chem . Affinity; adhesion; attraction.
subs: v.n. To act in unison or concert, to act together, act simultaneously; to combine, unite, conspire; ekā-ek, ekā-ekī, adv. All at once, simultaneously; suddenly.

Equal; uniform; of the same fashion. ek āṅkh dekhnā , v. n. To look with the same eye. ek andāz se , adv. Equally; on the same scale. ek aur , adj. Another; one more. ek ek, ek ek kar-ke, ek ke bād ek , adv. One by one; one after the other; consecutively; seriatim ; individually; each; every; apiece; severally; singly. 1. Ek ek kauṛī ko tarastā hai . 2. Chār chor, chaurāsī banye, ek ek kar-ke lūṭā . Prov. Four thieves plundered eighty-four banyās (a timid caste) one after the other. 3. Mastī meṅ ek ek pe girtā hai ek ek . Bedil. ek ek ke do do , Twice as much; double; hundred per cent. ek bāt , n. f. lit . One word. 1. The same thing; no difference; no matter. Sab ek bāt hai . It is all one. 2. One price; no variation or bargaining. Hamāre hāṅ ek bāt hai . We have but one price. ek bār ; Rus. ek bar, ik biryāṅ , adv. Once; once upon a time.

adj. Alike; similar; corresponding; matching. ek se ek , E.;— — — chaṛhtā, yā baṛhtā , W., adj. lit . One more than the other. Exceeding; excelling; surpassing. Dunyā meṅ ek se ek chaṛhtā hai. ek-se din na rahnā , v. n. Not to remain constant. Applied to a change of condition, most commonly from bad to good, but sometimes the reverse. Sadā ek-se din nahin rahte . Prov. Saying. ek shakhs , n. A certain individual or person. ek-tarfah , adj. One-sided; partial; ex parte ; biassed; prejudiced. ek-tarfah ḍigrī , n. One-sided decree. ek-faslī , adj. Yielding but one crop annually. ek ke pīchhe ek , adv. One after the other; one following the other; successively; continually; in a train. ek kī das sunānā , v. a. lit . To give back ten (words) for one. To pour down a shower of abuse; to pay back with interest. ek ke do karnā , v. a. To bisect; to divide into two equal parts; cut into two. ek-qalam ; P. yak-qalam , adv. lit . One pen. At a stroke; at once; entirely; altogether ( bilkul ). ek lāṭhī hāṅknā , v. n. lit . To drive with one stick. To rule all men with one rod; to use or treat indiscriminately. Gadhe, ghoṛe ko ek lāṭhī hāṅkte ho! Prov. What! use the same stick on a horse and a donkey! ek mā bāp ke , adj. Of the same parents; the whole blood. ek mā bāp ke honā , v. n. 1. To live as brethren; live in peace and good will. 2. To sing in harmony; sing in chorus; chime with. Ek mā bāp ke ho jāo . Players. ek muāmlah, ghar kī bāt , A domestic affair; a transaction between friends.

One thousand Rupees. ek dūs're kī madad , Co-operation with one another; mutual help. ek-de'sī; P. ham watan , adj.
